title: Update bundled takt SKILL.md with bead introspection CLI surface (history / --field / list filters)
description:
The takt SKILL.md (the bundled instructions agents read when working in any takt project) does not mention the bead introspection commands shipped today as part of feature B-e54ece26 / commit 81e29913. Agents in cookbook-app and other takt projects will keep falling back to inline `cat .takt/beads/X.json | python3 -c '...'` patterns until the SKILL.md teaches them the new commands.

The new CLI surface (already on main, currently undocumented in SKILL.md):

- `uv run takt bead history <id>` — formatted execution_history view (one line per entry); supports `--limit`, `--event`, `--json`, `--plain`
- `uv run takt bead show <id> --field PATH` — jq-like dotted-path projection (e.g. `--field status`, `--field handoff_summary.completed`, `--field execution_history[-1].event`)
- `uv run takt bead list --status STATUS` — filter beads by lifecycle status (repeatable, OR within `--status`, AND with other filters)
- `uv run takt bead list --agent AGENT` — filter by assigned agent type (repeatable)
- `uv run takt bead list --feature-root <id>` — scope listing to a single feature tree

Confirmation that this works on this main: `uv run takt bead show B-0b10d599 --field labels` returns the labels array as JSON. The feature is live; the docs gap is the only thing missing.

## Required edits

ONE file: `src/agent_takt/_data/claude_skills/takt/SKILL.md` (the bundled source of truth that ships in the wheel and gets installed into other projects via `takt init` / `takt upgrade`).

Two sections to update:

### Section 1 — "Status and inspection" (currently lines 41-53)

Replace the existing code block with an extended version that includes the new commands. Suggested content:

```bash
uv run takt summary                          # counts + next actionable beads
uv run takt summary --feature-root <id>     # scoped to one feature
uv run takt bead list --plain               # all beads as table
uv run takt bead list --label <label>       # filter by label (repeatable, AND)
uv run takt bead list --status <status>     # filter by lifecycle status (repeatable, OR)
uv run takt bead list --agent <agent>       # filter by agent type (repeatable, OR)
uv run takt bead list --feature-root <id>   # scope to one feature tree
uv run takt bead show <id>                  # full bead JSON
uv run takt bead show <id> --field <path>   # project a single field (e.g. status, handoff_summary.completed)
uv run takt bead history <id>               # formatted execution history (one line per entry)
uv run takt bead history <id> --limit 5     # last N entries
uv run takt bead history <id> --event failed --event retried   # filter by event type (repeatable)
uv run takt bead graph                      # Mermaid diagram of all beads
uv run takt bead graph --feature-root <id> # scoped to one feature
uv run takt bead graph --output graph.md   # write diagram to file
uv run takt tui                             # interactive terminal UI
```

Add a short paragraph immediately after the code block explaining when to use `--field` vs full `bead show`:

> Use `bead show <id> --field <path>` whenever you only need a specific value (status, handoff verdict, last event, block reason). It avoids the full JSON dump and is the canonical replacement for `cat .takt/beads/<id>.json | python3 -c "import json, sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d['handoff_summary']['completed'])"` and similar inline-parsing patterns. The `--field` syntax supports nested paths (`handoff_summary.verdict`) and array indexing including negatives (`execution_history[-1].event`).

### Section 2 — Add a new "Reading bead state" subsection (place it immediately after "Status and inspection")

```markdown
### Reading bead state

When you need to inspect a bead's state, prefer the dedicated CLI commands over reading `.takt/beads/<id>.json` directly or shelling out to `python3 -c '...'` for JSON parsing:

| Goal | Use |
|------|-----|
| Lifecycle log (chronological) | `uv run takt bead history <id>` |
| One specific field | `uv run takt bead show <id> --field <path>` |
| Full JSON for piping | `uv run takt bead show <id>` |
| Beads matching status / agent | `uv run takt bead list --status ... --agent ... --plain` |
| Beads in one feature tree | `uv run takt bead list --feature-root <id> --plain` |

`--field` paths use Python-style dotted access with bracket-style array indexing including negative indices: `--field handoff_summary.verdict`, `--field execution_history[-1].event`, `--field expected_files[0]`. Missing paths exit non-zero with a clear stderr message; null values exit zero with an empty line (legitimate "field unset"). Lists and dicts render as pretty JSON; scalars render bare without quotes — clean for shell capture.
```

## What this fix does NOT change

1. Does NOT modify `.claude/skills/takt/SKILL.md` or `.agents/skills/takt/SKILL.md`. Those are installed copies — they get refreshed via `takt upgrade` once the new `_data/` content ships in a release. Editing them directly would create drift.
2. Does NOT modify CLAUDE.md. The previous CLI ergonomics docs bead already updated CLAUDE.md correctly (verifiable via `grep "bead history" CLAUDE.md`).
3. Does NOT modify any other skill file (`role/`, `capability/`, `task/` skills). The new CLI is a takt-CLI feature, not a role-specific one.
4. Does NOT change the behavior of any command. Pure documentation.
5. Does NOT add tests. SKILL.md is markdown documentation; the CLI commands themselves already have full test coverage.

## Acceptance criteria

1. `src/agent_takt/_data/claude_skills/takt/SKILL.md` "Status and inspection" code block lists all new commands (`bead history`, `bead show --field`, `bead list --status`, `bead list --agent`, `bead list --feature-root`).
2. A new "Reading bead state" subsection exists immediately after "Status and inspection" with the goal-to-command mapping table and `--field` syntax explanation above.
3. `grep -E "bead history|--field|--status |--agent |--feature-root" src/agent_takt/_data/claude_skills/takt/SKILL.md` returns multiple hits (currently returns only the existing `summary --feature-root` and `graph --feature-root` mentions).
4. No other files modified.
5. `uv run pytest tests/ -n auto -q` passes (sanity check; no test impact expected for a docs-only change).

## References

- `src/agent_takt/_data/claude_skills/takt/SKILL.md` — the file to modify
- CLAUDE.md "Working with Beads" section — already updated with the same commands; use it as a content reference
- spec at `specs/done/bead-introspection-cli-ergonomics.md` (or wherever it landed) — full spec for the underlying feature
- Feature merge: commit 81e29913 (`Merge feature/b-e54ece26`)
